channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Stepmother accused of holding Connecticut man captive for over 20 years due in court
item.description: The stepmother accused of holding a man captive for most of his life in Waterbury is expected in court Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, KREX 5

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Palestinians chanted against Hamas during anti-war protests in the Gaza Strip, witnesses said Wednesday, in a rare show of public anger against the militant group that has long repressed dissent and still rules the territory 17 months into the war with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Atlantic releases the entire Signal chat showing Hegseth's detailed attack plans against Houthis
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Atlantic released the entire Signal chat among Trump senior national security officials Wednesday, showing that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth provided the exact timing of warplane launches and when bombs would drop — before the men and women flying those attacks against Yemen’
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A new Chili’s near Scranton will be a throwback to 'The Office,' 'awesome blossom' and all
item.description: It has been nearly two decades since the workplace mockumentary "The Office” first set an episode inside a Chili's, where Michael Scott handed out Dundie awards to his ever-tolerant employees, including trophies for the whitest sneakers and for stinking up the bathroom.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Atlantic publishes Trump Cabinet group chat messages
item.description: The Atlantic has published the Signal group chat messages among national security leaders that were inadvertently shared with Editor-in-Chief Jeffrey Goldberg, noting administration officials said Tuesday they were not classified.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Founders of Black-owned brands adapt their hopes and business plans for a post-DEI era
item.description: The Black creators of some consumers products are rethinking their business plans as major U.S. companies end or water down their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: Anne D'Innocenzio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Researchers in limbo as Columbia bows to Trump’s demands in bid to restore $400M federal funding
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When President Donald Trump canceled $400 million in funding to Columbia University over its handling of student protests against Israel's war in Gaza, much of the financial pain fell on researchers a train ride away from the school's campus, working on things like curing cancer and
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Utah adds protections for child influencers following YouTuber Ruby Franke's child abuse conviction
item.description: Utah on Tuesday added new protections for the children of online content creators following the child abuse conviction of Ruby Franke, a mother of six who dispensed parenting advice to millions on YouTube before her arrest in 2023.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A year after the Baltimore bridge collapse, a long road to recovery is ahead
item.description: A year after the catastrophic collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, Maryland leaders are honoring the six construction workers killed that night.
item.dc:creator: Lea Skene And Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former US attorney Jessica Aber suspected to have died from 'natural causes': police
item.description: The former U.S. attorney who was found dead in Alexandria, Virginia, over the weekend is suspected to have died from "natural causes," according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pilot and 2 young daughters survive the night on airplane wing after crashing into icy Alaska lake
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — A pilot and his two young daughters survived on the wing of a plane for about 12 hours after it crashed and was partially submerged in an icy Alaska lake, then were rescued after being spotted by a good Samaritan.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iowa law banning school library books that depict sex acts on hold again after a new federal ruling
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa cannot, for now, continue to enforce part of its book ban law, a federal judge said Tuesday, giving major publishers that sued the state the second temporary reprieve they requested. The new decision from U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs order seeking to overhaul US elections, including requiring proof of citizenship
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed a sweeping executive action to overhaul elections in the U.S., including requiring documentary proof of citizenship to register to vote in federal elections and demanding that all ballots be received by Election Day. The order says the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Columbia student protester can't be detained for now as she fights deportation, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Columbia University student from South Korea who is facing potential deportation for her involvement in a pro-Palestinian protest can't be taken into immigration detention for now, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate confirms Trump's nominee to run NIH
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday confirmed health researcher Jay Bhattacharya as the next leader of the National Institutes of Health.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Family of Riley Strain files wrongful death lawsuit against Delta Chi fraternity
item.description: Family members of Riley Strain, a University of Missouri student who died last year during a group trip to Nashville, Tennessee, have filed a wrongful death lawsuit against his former fraternity and pledge brothers.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wealthy coastal enclave nanny charged with manslaughter in toddler's death
item.description: Aimee Cotton, a babysitter on Martha's Vineyard, allegedly left small children unattended in her vehicle for three hours, leading to the death of one child.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Slim chance for northern lights in some states due to 'high speed stream' from Sun
item.description: (NEXSTAR) — Some across the U.S. may again have a chance to see the northern lights Tuesday night as material hurled out by the sun reaches Earth.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The US Postal Service has been struggling for years. Now Trump's talking about privatizing it
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service is facing an uncertain future after the resignation this week of Postmaster General Louis DeJoy and the suggestion by President Donald Trump and Elon Musk, who heads the Department of Government Efficiency, that the mail service could be privatized.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump orders declassification of FBI’s Russia investigation
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed a memorandum directing the declassification of FBI files related to the investigation into his 2016 campaign's potential ties to Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Atlantic editor suggests he's open to sharing Hegseth's full war plans texts publicly
item.description: Goldberg reported he saw in a message, sent to the group by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, specific weapons systems, human targets and other top secret information.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Another camouflaged camera found in yard of Los Angles home
item.description: This is at least the fourth hidden camera discovery in March alone, coming just days after another hidden camera was recovered after a home was burglarized.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: United Airlines flight to China diverted to San Francisco after pilot forgets passport
item.description: A United Airlines flight headed to Shanghai, China, had to turn around over the weekend after the pilot forgot to bring a passport, the airline company said.
item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mega Millions is changing: 7 things to know before April's price change, game overhaul
item.description: More expensive tickets, faster-growing jackpots, and a built-in multiplier — here's what to expect when Mega Millions gets overhauled next month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Student returns lost backpack filled with luxury jewelry, diamonds, cash
item.description: Amid the fog of international travel, a group of Jewish students studying in Jerusalem was handed a backpack -- that none recognized as their own.
item.dc:creator: Anthony DiLorenzo, KRON4

item.title: Wildfires in North and South Carolina fueled by drought, wind and fallen trees from Hurricane Helene
item.description: Dry conditions, wind and trees downed by Hurricane Helene are fueling wildfires in North and South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Consider deleting your 23andMe data now, California AG urges
item.description: The genetic testing company has the genetic data of more than15 million customers — and California Attorney General Rob Bonta has advised users to purge their data.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: Trump portrait to be removed from Colorado Capitol after president's criticism
item.description: President Trump has criticized a portrait of himself hanging in the Colorado Capitol, suggesting it was intentionally distorted, while Republican lawmakers have asked for it to be removed.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KRON4

item.title: US northern border sees Romanian illegal immigrant influx as expert predicts what could be driving them
item.description: An immigration expert says visa policies in Canada attract foreigners, who then illegally enter the United States via the northern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jeffries calls for investigation of top Trump officials after bombshell military breach
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) is calling for Congress to investigate top Trump administration officials in the wake of a bombshell revelation that The Atlantic’s top editor was included in a group chat discussion detailing the Pentagon’s plans to strike Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Maps: The states, counties with the most excessive drinkers, per new report
item.description: "Excessive drinking" includes binge drinking, heavy drinking, drinking while pregnant, and underage drinking, according to the CDC.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Japan's cherry blossom season begins as first blooms appear in Tokyo
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan 's official cherry blossom spotters on Monday confirmed the first blooming of the country's favorite flower, declaring the official start of the festive season in the Japanese capital.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pope returns to Vatican after 5-week hospital stay over life-threatening bout of pneumonia
item.description: A weak and frail Pope Francis returned home to the Vatican from the hospital on Sunday after surviving a five-week, life-threatening bout of pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Major League Baseball removes references to 'diversity' from MLB Careers home page
item.description: Baseball Commissioner Rob Manfred, who launched a Diversity Pipeline Program in 2016, said following an owners meeting in Palm Beach, Florida, last month that MLB was evaluating the interpretation of law coming from the federal government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Florida man is executed for the killings of an 8-year-old girl and her grandmother
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man who killed an 8-year-old girl and her grandmother on a night in which he drank heavily and used drugs was executed Thursday evening. Prison officials said Edward James, 63, was pronounced dead at 8:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, WGN 9
